# Roam Live

> Free open-source Android app for IRL streaming. Works with Twitch, Kick, YouTube, and any RTMP server.

## About

Roam Live is a hobbyist-built Android app for streaming from a phone to live streaming platforms. It's designed for IRL (In Real Life) streaming: walking around with the phone clipped to a vest, on a gimbal, or held in hand, streaming to Twitch, Kick, YouTube, or any RTMP-compatible service.

It's deliberately platform-agnostic. The streamer pastes their full RTMP URL (server + key) and Roam pushes to whatever's on the other end. No Twitch-specific or Kick-specific code paths.

## Key features

- Works with any RTMP / RTMPS server (no platform lock-in)
- Native chat panel showing Twitch and Kick chat side by side, visible only to the streamer and not burned into the broadcast
- Auto-reconnect when the network drops mid-stream, with configurable max retry time
- Stealth mode: black screen for discretion in public, stream keeps going
- BRB screen with custom text, one-tap mute of camera and audio
- Torch / flashlight toggle for night streams
- Predictive thermal management: warns the streamer and reduces bitrate before the phone overheats and the OS kills the stream

## Privacy

- No telemetry, no analytics, no tracking
- Google Play Services not required to run
- Talks only to the streaming server the user points it at
- Open source under GPL-3.0 so the code can be audited

## Technical

- Platform: Android 10 and up (Android 14+ recommended for full feature set)
- License: GPL-3.0
- Source code: https://github.com/whitspce/roamlive
- Website: https://roamlive.app/

## Distribution

- Free, no payment required
- Distributed as APK directly from GitHub releases (not on the Play Store, by design)
- Optional donations supported via Stripe (card) and Bitcoin: https://roamlive.app/support/

## Status

Early alpha. The core stream-from-phone path works reliably; expect bugs and missing features in less-trodden paths. Feedback is wanted, send anything to feedback@roamlive.app or open a GitHub issue.

## Comparison

People sometimes ask how Roam Live compares to:

- **Streamlabs Mobile**: Streamlabs supports more platforms out of the box and has more integrations, but is closed-source, heavy on battery, and prone to overheating phones. Roam is lighter, runs cooler, and is fully open-source, with a deliberately smaller feature set focused on doing the streaming part well.
- **Larix Broadcaster**: Larix is the closest peer (also Android, also RTMP-agnostic). Larix is more configurable for power users (more codec options, SRT support, network bonding). Roam is more opinionated and simpler, with built-in chat reading and stealth mode that Larix doesn't have.
- **The official Twitch / Kick mobile apps**: those only stream to their own platform. Roam streams to any RTMP server. The official Kick app, as of this writing, also lacks chat reading on screen, BRB, torch toggle, and stealth mode, all of which Roam has.
